course_title: Leading and Managing the Future in Pharmacy Practice
course_description:
This course provides skills in pharmacy management and leadership, including designing and leading service initiatives and change management in hospital and community settings. Students will gain insight into leadership, management and organisational dynamics, quality assurance in health systems, and human resource management basics, offering them skills and knowledge for advancing their career in the future.

The course is delivered as three online modules.

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:
restrictions: This course is restricted to students enrolled in the Graduate Certificate/Masters of Clinical Pharmacy and Graduate Certificate in Pharmacy Practice

timetable:
Additional timetable information
Please see Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for timetable information. Tutorial dates may differ based on the group you are allocated to.
course_aims: This course will further develop students’ understanding and application of strategic planning and management, and leadership principles and theories to drive change and initiatives in their practice, including supporting organisational change, and effective teams. At the end of the course, students will be able to undertake comprehensive business and service planning for a pharmacy service (or initiatives) within their practice environment.

learning_outcomes.description: Design and evaluate pharmacy services that address value-based care and organisational objectives.
Apply strategic planning principles to the development, resourcing, and implementation of pharmacy services within the healthcare system.

Develop and communicate a strategic business plan that aligns with healthcare priorities, demonstrates financial viability, and incorporates effective marketing strategies to engage stakeholders and support implementation.
Apply leadership and team management strategies to foster collaboration, and enhance workforce performance through recruitment, staff induction, and performance appraisal processes.
Analyse and address organisational challenges to change management to ensure the sustainability and impact of pharmacy services.

assessment_details.title: Business or Service Development Plan (Plan on 2 pages)
assessment_details.task_description: Outline the resourcing, activities, and outputs required to implement a new business initiative or pharmacy service for your professional practice setting, and the resulting desired outcomes and impact using a Logic Model (max. 2 x A4 pages, using at least 8-point font size). See the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for task details and the marking criteria. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) technologies, successful completion of the assessment will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students may be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. assessment_details.title: Tutorial assessments (Group presentations and Elevator Pitch)
assessment_details.due_date: Group presentation Module-1 24/03/2026 - 24/03/2026 Elevator Pitch 27/04/2026 - 29/04/2026 Group presentation Module-3 26/05/2026 - 26/05/2026 Due on: Week 5 - Tutorial 1, Week 9 - Tutorial 2 and Week 13 for Tutorial 3
assessment_details.task_description: There will be two parts associated with this assessment. This will include two group presentations (at Tutorial 1, and Tutorial 3) and an individual presentation of an elevator pitch of your business initiative at Tutorial 2. Details shown below: Part 1- Group Presentations (Tutorial 1 and 3) As a group you will prepare and present a presentation on a given task at the specified tutorials. The course coordinator will assess the presentations during online tutorials (T1 and T3). The coordinator and a guest will provide feedback on the presentations and there will be group discussion around learnings from each presentation. Two presenters per group per tutorial (group list in the Communications section). All group members MUST contribute to preparation of the materials for presentation and will receive the same marks if they have contributed meaningfully to the task. All group members should work collaboratively to respond to questions. Upload slides for your presentation in your specified group discussion board (using slide deck/outlook). Ensure the group signs the declaration (see attached) regarding the contributions of each group member. Length: Maximum 6 minutes Submission: Upload to your group Discussion Board via Learn.UQ. Present your group work at your tutorial and receive feedback and questions from other groups, and course coordinator Deadline: Upload slides to your group Discussion Board on the morning of the Module 1 and Module 3 tutorials Weight: 5% for each group presentation See Learn.UQ for details of the group tutorial presentations Part 2- Individual Elevator Pitch (Tutorial 2) For this assessment, you will develop a concise and persuasive elevator pitch outlining your business initiative or pharmacy service. The purpose of this task is to convince a key stakeholder of the value and impact of your proposal within a limited timeframe. This tutorial and all elevator pitch presentations will be recorded for review and marking purposes. Key Requirements Length: Between 4 to 5 minutes (the assessor will stop marking after 5 minutes - i.e. maximum 5 minutes). Submission: Present your pitch at your allocated online tutorial and receive feedback from your peers, course coordinator and expert guest (a pharmacy leader). Deadline: Module 2 Tutorial. Weighting: 20% of your final grade. Technical Considerations: Ensure clear audio, and students must have their camera on along with the slides to ensure it is clear that you are presenting the work and responding to questions (for verification of identity). Task Instructions Your elevator pitch should: explain your proposed initiative or pharmacy service, to convince the listener of why your initiative or service would be productive/beneficial to implement/support in your professional practice setting. Introduction: Provide a very brief introduction of yourself and your professional role. Business Initiative or Service Proposal: Clearly describe your proposed initiative or service. Problem Statement & Justification: Identify the problem your initiative addresses and its purpose. Justify why this initiative is needed within your professional setting. Potential Impact: Explain how your initiative will benefit stakeholders and contribute to the healthcare system. Call to Action: Conclude with a clear and actionable request (e.g., funding, support, policy change). Evaluation : How will you evaluate the new service model if it is implemented? There is no strict sequence for presenting these elements, but your pitch should be engaging, structured, and compelling. See the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for details and the marking criteria. Use of AI and Machine Translation Technologies: These tasks have been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) technologies, successful completion of the assessment will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students may be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description

Submit via the link provided on Learn.UQ (Blackboard).
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
No discretionary extensions permitted for the tutorial presentations and pitch.
You can request an extension on behalf of your group for a group assessment item, provided that at least 50% of your group members also agree to the request for an extension.

learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 5 (23 Feb - 29 Mar)
learning_activities.topic: Module 1 One online tutorial will be held for this module on a Tuesday evening of Week 5, 6-8 PM (AEST i.e. Brisbane time). See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for the timetable details and your groups. Module 1: The Australian Healthcare System; Leadership vs Management and Strategic Planning; Development of Services (or Initiatives); Quality Assurance and Quality Improvement of Services (or Initiatives) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

Course Outline An introductory online tutorial will be held in Week 1 of semester - Tuesday 6-7 PM (AEST i.e. Brisbane time). This will provide an overview of course, assessment, and resources that are available for your use. Learning outcomes: L01

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 6 To Week 9 (30 Mar - 03 May)
learning_activities.topic: Module 2 One online tutorial will be held for this module on the Monday, Tuesday, or Wednesday evening of Week 9, depending on your group, from 6-8 PM (AEST i.e. Brisbane time). See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for the timetable and groups. Module 2: Elevator Pitch, Strategic Management; Marketing; Finances and Budget Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 10 To Week 13 (04 May - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Module 3 One online tutorial will be held for this module on Tuesday of Week 13, 6-8 PM (AEST i.e. Brisbane time). See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for the timetable. Module 3: Organisational Change Management; Leadership and Culture; High-Performing Teams; Recruitment, Staff Induction, Performance Appraisal Processes; Workforce Well-being: Motivation and Burnout Learning outcomes: L04, L05

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
